## Localized dialog + tighter agent

v0.5 makes the Sentinel dialog feel native in your language and
hardens the polkit-agent code paths that v0.4 introduced.

## i18n — twelve languages out of the box

The helper's UI chrome (Allow / Deny / Show details / Auto-deny
countdown / detail row labels / truncation marker) is now translated.
Twelve locales ship embedded in the binary — no separate
`/usr/share/sentinel/locales/` install, just bigger binaries by ~60 KB
total:

`en-US`, `de-DE`, `es-ES`, `fr-FR`, `it-IT`, `ja-JP`, `nl-NL`,
`pl-PL`, `pt-BR`, `ru-RU`, `tr-TR`, `zh-CN`.

Locale resolution: `LC_ALL → LC_MESSAGES → LANG → en-US`. POSIX
forms like `tr_TR.UTF-8@modifier` are canonicalized + strictly
validated (`[A-Za-z0-9-]{1,16}`) before negotiation against the
embedded set. Anything malformed silently falls back to `en-US` —
translation is best-effort and never blocks auth.

For the PAM-spawned dialog path (sudo, pkexec via PAM), the locale
env vars are recovered from `/proc/<requesting_pid>/environ` since
sudo/polkit-agent-helper-1 scrub them. Values are validated against
a strict whitelist before forwarding (defense in depth — `/proc`
content is user-controlled).

If the admin still has the default `[appearance].title/message/
secondary` in `/etc/security/sentinel.conf`, those auto-translate
too. Custom strings pass through verbatim — admin's text is
admin's text.

## Dialog now actually renders message + secondary

Pre-existing bug discovered while wiring i18n: `--message` and
`--secondary` were accepted as CLI args but never displayed in the
dialog. Now rendered between the title and the process card
(message body) and just above the buttons (secondary instruction
line).

## Process icon

The dialog now shows an icon for the requesting executable when the
icon theme has a match for its basename (UAC-style). Resolved once
at app init via cosmic's icon theme machinery; if no theme entry
exists we render no icon rather than a generic placeholder.

For `pkexec foo` flows the requesting process is often the shell or
a CLI tool (`bash`, `topgrade`) without an icon — that's fine, the
dialog renders without one. Icons appear naturally for GUI apps that
trigger polkit auths (gnome-software, discover, etc.).

## Agent: critical correctness fixes

Three bugs from v0.4 that wouldn't have shown up in single-user
testing but were waiting:

- **`CancelAuthentication` actually cancels now.** The previous
  code inserted the auth's `JoinHandle` into a map then immediately
  removed it before awaiting, so polkit's cancel signal found
  nothing to abort. Replaced with a `tokio::sync::oneshot` so the
  handle stays in the map for the entire auth and `.abort()`
  works.
- **Approval-queue race window bounded.** TTL dropped from 5 s to
  1 s, plus a per-Agent serialization mutex held across the entirety
  of `BeginAuthentication`. Two unrelated polkit auths within 1 s
  can no longer mis-pair.
- **`polkit-agent-helper-1` calls are now bounded.** A 10 s timeout
  wraps the `/run/polkit/agent-helper.socket` interaction so a
  wedged helper-1 returns `FAILURE` instead of hanging the agent
  task forever.

## Agent now reads the config file

In v0.4 the agent's dialog hardcoded title / timeout / randomize /
message defaults — `/etc/security/sentinel.conf`'s `[appearance]`
and `[services.polkit-1]` blocks were ignored. Fixed: the agent
loads config per polkit auth (same per-call discipline as the PAM
module) and respects every key except `enabled`. Edit the file,
next polkit auth uses the new value, no agent restart required.

`enabled = false` for `polkit-1` is logged but not honored — the
agent has already registered with polkitd, so refusing now would
leave polkit with no agent at all.

## Structured audit logs

Auth-outcome events now emit `logfmt`-style key=value lines so
`journalctl -t pam_sentinel -t sentinel-polkit-agent -o json` is
trivially queryable. Latency per dialog is captured (the journal
doesn't naturally have decision-latency data — this puts it in
without a separate database).

```text
event=auth.allow source=dialog user=alice service=sudo process=cat uid=1000 latency_ms=1234
event=auth.allow source=agent user=alice action=org.freedesktop.policykit.exec process=topgrade latency_ms=2376
event=auth.allow source=bypass uid=1000
event=auth.deny  source=agent user=alice action=org.freedesktop.policykit.exec process=bash latency_ms=587
```

## Layer-shell auto-downgrade on GNOME

Mutter doesn't implement `zwlr-layer-shell-v1`, so v0.4 hard-failed
on GNOME / Pantheon / Budgie / Unity. v0.5 detects this via
`XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP` and auto-falls back to `--windowed` mode (a
regular `xdg-toplevel` window instead of the full-screen overlay).
A one-line stderr notice prints when the fallback kicks in. Force
either mode with `--layer-shell` or `--windowed`.

## Install: no relogin required

`pkexec ./install.sh` (or distro package upgrade running the same
flow) now restarts the running agent in place — sessionid sanity
check, SIGTERM, then re-spawn the freshly-installed binary fully
detached via `setsid -f setpriv` so the new agent ends up directly
under PID 1 with a clean process tree. The old "log out and back
in to activate" guidance is gone for in-place upgrades; only fresh
installs need the relogin.

## Other changes

- **Internal restructuring**: schema, parser, and `format_message`
  extracted into a shared `sentinel-config` crate consumed by both
  the PAM module and the agent. The agent finally uses the same
  config types as PAM instead of duplicating defaults.
- **`%u`/`%s`/`%p` substitution applied to the title too** — was
  silently dropped before.
- **Malformed config logs a warning** instead of silently
  reverting to defaults (`pam_sentinel: failed to parse
  /etc/security/sentinel.conf: …`).
- **`sm_setcred` returns `PAM_IGNORE`** instead of falsely
  claiming to manage credentials.
- **`comm_matches` for the bypass socket** tightened from
  `starts_with("polkit-agent-")` to exact-match-only, defense in
  depth on top of `SO_PEERCRED`.
- **`libcosmic` pinned** to a specific commit instead of tracking
  `branch = "master"` — reproducible builds for a security-adjacent
  crate.
- **CI workflow** added: fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, workspace
  tests, release build on every PR.
- **`uninstall.sh` fallback list** completed — previously left 8
  files (man pages and shell completions) behind when the install
  state file was missing.

## Verify

```bash
pgrep -af sentinel-polkit-agent
pkexec true                                # one Sentinel dialog
journalctl -t sentinel-polkit-agent -t pam_sentinel \
    --output=cat --since "1 minute ago"    # see structured event= lines

# Try a different language without logging out:
kill $(pgrep -f sentinel-polkit-agent)
LANG=de_DE.UTF-8 setsid -f /usr/lib/sentinel-polkit-agent
pkexec true                                # German dialog
```

Full installation, configuration, architecture, and troubleshooting
docs at <https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki>.
